description: Nigerian petroleum subsidy regime timeline — major policy events with political significance.

sources_used.name: Wikipedia — Petroleum industry in Nigeria (2012 crisis)

data.event: January 2012 subsidy removal and Occupy Nigeria protests
data.pump_price_notes: Wikipedia: fuel increased from ~$1.70/gallon to ~$3.50/gallon (approx NGN65 to NGN141 per litre at then-exchange-rate)
data.political_outcome: Occupy Nigeria protests; Jonathan administration partially reversed the removal to NGN97/litre after negotiations with NLC/TUC

data.pump_price_notes: Buhari administration raised regulated pump price from NGN87 to NGN145 in May 2016 — described as partial deregulation
data.political_outcome: NLC protests; fuel scarcity; no full strike action

data.event: Tinubu inaugural address — full fuel subsidy removal
data.pump_price_notes: Price rapidly increased to ~NGN500-600/litre immediately post-announcement; later rose further as naira devalued. By late 2024 prices exceeded NGN1,000/litre in many locations.
data.political_outcome: NLC called for strike; negotiations; partial cash transfers announced as palliatives. Became Tinubu's most politically costly early decision.

data.event: CBN removes FX trading restrictions — naira float
data.naira_per_usd_notes: Naira fell to historic lows. Official rate converged with parallel rate. By late 2024 naira was ~NGN1,600-1,700/USD.
data.political_outcome: Import cost surge; worsened inflation; praised by IMF/World Bank but deeply unpopular with ordinary Nigerians.

gaps.note: Monthly PMS pump prices from NNPCL or NBS not retrieved.
Multiple subsidy-related price adjustments 2016-2022 not fully documented here. Source: NNPCL press releases or NBS.
